title: feat: restore copy model slug on models page
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
Restores one-click copy of a model's API id (slug). The slug was the persistent grey line under each name dropped in 8130299bb to de-noise the table — that line was the only way to copy it, so copy went with it. This brings copy back without the line.

- Model name is now the copy trigger — hover shows `Copy "flux"` (also lets you read the slug), click copies and flashes a `copied` badge
- Description moves off the name's hover onto an `ⓘ` info icon; opens on hover/click/focus, so it's reachable on touch (it wasn't shown on mobile before)
- Mobile: the name is a `pointer-events-auto` tap island that copies; chevron/logo/chips/price/whitespace still expand the row
- Reuses the existing `@pollinations/ui` `CopyButton` + `InfoTip` — no new components, no slug line back

biome and `tsc --noEmit` clean.

gist.headline: Copy that, slug
gist.blurb: We brought back the ability to one-click copy model API slugs from the models page. Just click a model's name to grab its ID, keeping the table clean and your workflow fast.
gist.summary: Restored one-click copying of model API slugs on the models page by making the model name a clickable copy trigger, and moved model descriptions to a dedicated info icon.
gist.impact: Users can easily copy model IDs directly from the table without the UI feeling cluttered, and mobile users can now access model descriptions via the new info tooltip.
gist.visual_concept: The bee mascot interacting with a clean, organized list or table, copying a specific item. The monitor robot holding a neatly organized list. Focus on UI cleanliness and the action of 'copying' data.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing tiny reading glasses, happily using a small glowing stamp on a clean retro computer terminal screen. A chunky monitor robot stands nearby holding a neatly organized list. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Text in pixel font: 'COPY'. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley, chunky sprites, emotionally warm.
